sourceMovie.title: I Want You
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: I Want You (2012) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Fernando González Molina's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: After
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I Want You clicked for you, After (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after I Want You?
faq.answer: Try After, After We Collided, The Illusionist—each shares DNA with I Want You while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I Want You?
faq.answer: I Want You (2012) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I Want You part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Three Steps Above Heaven Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
